description: Phase 1: CRM + Estimating module — test-first development with full CRUD, pipeline management, estimate builder, ERPNext mock sync, and missing table infrastructure for existing pages

userStories.title: Test infrastructure: mock factories and test helpers
userStories.description: Create reusable test utilities that all future test files will import. This avoids duplicating mock setup across 15+ test files. Build on the existing patterns in __tests__/api/projects.test.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/helpers/mock-supabase.ts exists — exports mockSupabaseClient() that returns a chainable mock with configurable responses per table
__tests__/helpers/mock-auth.ts exists — exports mockClerkAuth(userId?) that sets up vi.mock for @clerk/nextjs/server
__tests__/helpers/mock-request.ts exists — exports makeRequest(path, init?) and makeJsonRequest(path, body, method?) helpers

__tests__/helpers/fixtures.ts exists — exports factory functions: makeAccount(), makeContact(), makeLead(), makeOpportunity(), makeActivity(), makeEstimate(), makeEstimateLine() that return valid test data matching Zod schemas
__tests__/helpers/index.ts re-exports all helpers
Existing 22 tests still pass (npm run test)
Typecheck passes (npm run typecheck)
Lint passes (npm run lint)
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: This must be the first story. All subsequent API tests depend on these helpers. Study __tests__/api/projects.test.ts for the existing mockSupabaseChain pattern — generalize it. The mock Supabase client must support: .from(table).select().eq().order().limit() chains, .from(table).insert().select().single() chains, .from(table).update().eq().select().single() chains, .from(table).delete().eq() chains. Each should accept {data, error} to configure the response.

userStories.title: CRM + Operations schema migration
userStories.description: Create Supabase migration 00003_crm_operations.sql with all tables needed for CRM, Estimating, Projects, Expenses, Notifications, and ERP sync. Reference the canonical schema at docs/architecture/KrewPact-Backend-SQL-Schema-Draft.sql but adapt: drop FKs to tables we're NOT creating (contract_terms, file_folders, file_metadata, portal_accounts, assemblies, cost_catalog_items, file_folders) — make those columns UUID nullable without FK constraint. Include enums, indexes, triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/00003_crm_operations.sql exists
Enums created: lead_stage, opportunity_stage, estimate_status, project_status, task_status, expense_status, notification_channel, notification_state
CRM tables created: accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities, opportunity_stage_history
Estimating tables created: estimates, estimate_lines, estimate_versions
Project tables created: projects (contract_id nullable, no FK), project_members, milestones, tasks, task_comments, project_daily_logs
Expense table created: expense_claims (no FK to file_metadata)
Notification tables created: notifications (user_id FK only, no portal_accounts FK), notification_preferences
ERP bridge tables created: erp_sync_map, erp_sync_jobs, erp_sync_events, erp_sync_errors

All indexes from Resolution doc H6 section included (idx_projects_division, idx_estimates_division_status, idx_leads_division_stage, idx_accounts_division, idx_tasks_project_status, idx_milestones_project, idx_project_members_project, idx_project_members_user, idx_daily_logs_project_date, idx_contacts_account, idx_opportunities_account, idx_activities_opportunity, idx_estimate_lines_estimate, idx_estimates_opportunity, idx_erp_sync_map_entity, idx_erp_sync_jobs_status)
updated_at triggers for all tables with updated_at column
Migration applies successfully via supabase db push
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All existing 22 tests still pass
userStories.notes: Run: supabase db push to apply. Verify with: supabase gen types typescript --project-id owfjnfdqpzpvzvdobpxa 2>/dev/null | head -50 (should show new tables). COALESCE in unique constraints must use CREATE UNIQUE INDEX, not inline UNIQUE. RLS functions already exist in public schema from 00002.

userStories.title: RLS policies for CRM + operations tables
userStories.description: Create migration 00004_crm_rls_policies.sql with deny-by-default RLS for all new tables. Use existing helper functions from 00002 (public.krewpact_user_id(), public.krewpact_divisions(), public.is_platform_admin()). Write a comprehensive RLS test file that validates policy behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/00004_crm_rls_policies.sql exists
RLS ENABLED on ALL 20+ new tables from US-002

CRM tables (accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities, opportunity_stage_history): authenticated SELECT where division_id matches JWT krewpact_divisions claim, platform_admin bypasses. INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for own division + platform_admin.
Estimating tables (estimates, estimate_lines, estimate_versions): division-scoped. estimate_lines inherit access from parent estimate.
Project tables (projects, project_members, milestones, tasks, task_comments, project_daily_logs): project members can SELECT, project_manager+ can INSERT/UPDATE, platform_admin manages all
expense_claims: user can manage own (user_id match), accounting + platform_admin can view all
notifications + notification_preferences: user can only see/manage own (user_id match)
ERP sync tables (erp_sync_map, erp_sync_jobs, erp_sync_events, erp_sync_errors): platform_admin and operations_manager only
__tests__/rls/crm-policies.test.ts exists with at least 8 tests verifying RLS intent (test the SQL logic, not live DB)
Migration applies via supabase db push
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: Division isolation pattern: USING (division_id::text IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(public.krewpact_divisions()))). For project member access: USING (id IN (SELECT project_id FROM project_members WHERE user_id::text = public.krewpact_user_id()) OR public.is_platform_admin()). RLS test file should test the SQL helper functions return expected values given mock JWT claims.

userStories.title: Regenerate TypeScript types and wire typed Supabase clients
userStories.description: Regenerate types/supabase.ts from the live schema. Add Database type parameter to both Supabase client files. Write tests that verify the type generation includes all new tables.
acceptanceCriteria: types/supabase.ts regenerated with all tables from US-002 migration

types/supabase.ts contains interfaces for: accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities, opportunity_stage_history, estimates, estimate_lines, estimate_versions, projects, project_members, milestones, tasks, task_comments, project_daily_logs, expense_claims, notifications, notification_preferences, erp_sync_map, erp_sync_jobs, erp_sync_events, erp_sync_errors
lib/supabase/client.ts imports Database from types/supabase and passes as generic: createClient<Database>(...)
lib/supabase/server.ts imports Database and passes as generic: createClient<Database>(...)
__tests__/types/supabase-types.test.ts exists — verifies Database type exports Tables for all expected table names (compile-time check)
All existing API routes compile (fix column name mismatches if TypeScript errors appear)
Typecheck passes (npm run typecheck)
Lint passes
All existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: Run: supabase gen types typescript --project-id owfjnfdqpzpvzvdobpxa 2>/dev/null > types/supabase.ts. IMPORTANT: createClient<Database>() crashes tsc 5.9.3 with 'Map maximum size exceeded' when 28+ tables are in the Database type. Solution: call createClient() WITHOUT the generic — the __InternalSupabase field is present but auto-detection returns any. Database type is imported and re-exported for manual use. Explicit <Database> generic should be added when TypeScript fixes the Map size issue, or when a simpler type wrapper is created.

userStories.title: CRM Zod validators with comprehensive validation tests
userStories.description: Create Zod schemas for all CRM entities (Account, Contact, Lead, Opportunity, Activity). Write thorough validation tests BEFORE the API routes exist — these tests validate schema behavior in isolation. Tests cover: valid data passes, required fields missing fails, invalid types fail, edge cases (empty strings, max lengths, email format, enum values).
acceptanceCriteria: lib/validators/crm.ts exports: accountCreateSchema, accountUpdateSchema, contactCreateSchema, contactUpdateSchema, leadCreateSchema, leadUpdateSchema, leadStageTransitionSchema, opportunityCreateSchema, opportunityUpdateSchema, activityCreateSchema, activityUpdateSchema
lib/validators/crm.ts exports TypeScript types inferred from schemas: AccountCreate, ContactCreate, LeadCreate, etc.
accountCreateSchema requires: account_name (1-200 chars), account_type (string). Optional: division_id (uuid), billing_address (object), shipping_address (object), notes (string)
contactCreateSchema requires: first_name (1-100), last_name (1-100). Optional: account_id (uuid), email (valid email), phone, role_title, is_primary (boolean)
leadCreateSchema requires: lead_name (1-200). Optional: division_id (uuid), source, company_name, email (valid email), phone, estimated_value (>= 0), probability_pct (0-100), assigned_to (uuid)
leadStageTransitionSchema requires: stage (enum: new/qualified/estimating/proposal_sent/won/lost). Conditional: lost_reason required when stage=lost

opportunityCreateSchema requires: opportunity_name (1-200). Optional: lead_id, account_id, contact_id, division_id (all uuid), stage (enum), target_close_date, estimated_revenue (>= 0), probability_pct (0-100), owner_user_id (uuid)

activityCreateSchema requires: activity_type (call/email/meeting/note/task), title (1-200). Optional: opportunity_id, lead_id, account_id, contact_id (uuid), details, due_at, owner_user_id. Refinement: at least one of opportunity_id/lead_id/account_id/contact_id must be provided
__tests__/validators/crm.test.ts exists with at least 25 tests

Tests cover: valid creates pass, missing required fields fail, invalid email format fails, negative estimated_value fails, probability > 100 fails, lost stage without lost_reason fails, activity without any entity ID fails, account_name empty string fails, uuid format validation, enum value validation
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: Write ALL tests first. Run them — they should all pass since they test Zod schema validation (no API needed). This is pure validation logic. Use z.refine() for the lead stage lost_reason check and activity entity ID requirement.

userStories.title: Accounts + Contacts CRUD API routes with tests
userStories.description: Create API routes for Accounts and Contacts CRUD. Write failing route tests FIRST using test helpers from US-001, then implement the routes to make tests pass. Follow TDD: test → fail → implement → pass.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/crm/accounts.test.ts exists with at least 12 tests

Tests cover: GET returns 401 without auth, GET returns accounts list, GET with division_id filter, GET with search param, POST returns 401 without auth, POST creates account with valid data, POST returns 400 for invalid data (missing account_name), POST returns 400 for invalid division_id format, GET /[id] returns account, PATCH /[id] updates account, DELETE /[id] deletes account, GET /[id] returns 404 for non-existent account
__tests__/api/crm/contacts.test.ts exists with at least 10 tests

Tests cover: GET returns contacts list, GET with account_id filter, POST creates contact, POST with invalid email fails, GET /[id] returns contact, PATCH /[id] updates contact, DELETE /[id] deletes contact, POST without first_name fails, GET with search works, POST with is_primary flag works
app/api/crm/accounts/route.ts implements GET (list with division_id, search, limit, offset) and POST
app/api/crm/accounts/[id]/route.ts implements GET, PATCH, DELETE
app/api/crm/contacts/route.ts implements GET (list with account_id, search) and POST
app/api/crm/contacts/[id]/route.ts implements GET, PATCH, DELETE
All routes use Clerk auth guard, Zod validation from US-005, typed Supabase client
All 22+ new tests pass
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: TDD pattern: 1) Write test file importing from route paths that don't exist yet. 2) Create the route files. 3) Implement until tests pass. Use mockSupabaseClient() from __tests__/helpers/. Follow the exact pattern from __tests__/api/projects.test.ts. GOTCHA: TEST_IDS UUIDs (00000000-...) fail Zod strict UUID validation — use proper v4 UUIDs like 'a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a11' in query param tests.

userStories.title: Leads CRUD API with stage transitions and tests
userStories.description: Create API routes for Leads CRUD with stage transition endpoint. Stage transitions validate allowed paths and require lost_reason for lost stage. Write tests FIRST.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/crm/leads.test.ts exists with at least 18 tests

Tests cover: GET list returns leads, GET list filters by division_id, GET list filters by stage, GET list filters by assigned_to, GET list with search, POST creates lead with default stage 'new', POST with all optional fields, POST with invalid data returns 400, GET /[id] returns lead, PATCH /[id] updates lead, DELETE /[id] deletes lead

Stage transition tests: POST /[id]/stage transitions new→qualified, qualified→estimating, estimating→proposal_sent, proposal_sent→won, any→lost (with lost_reason), rejects new→won (skip), rejects lost→qualified (dead end), rejects lost without lost_reason, rejects invalid stage enum value
app/api/crm/leads/route.ts implements GET and POST
app/api/crm/leads/[id]/route.ts implements GET, PATCH, DELETE
app/api/crm/leads/[id]/stage/route.ts implements POST with transition validation
Stage transition logic defined in lib/crm/lead-stages.ts — exports ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS map and validateTransition(currentStage, newStage) function
lib/crm/lead-stages.ts has its own unit test: __tests__/lib/crm/lead-stages.test.ts with at least 8 tests for the pure transition logic
All new tests pass
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Separate the stage transition logic into a pure function (lib/crm/lead-stages.ts) so it can be tested without mocking. ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS should be a Record<lead_stage, lead_stage[]> map. The stage route should: 1) fetch current lead, 2) validate transition, 3) update lead stage, 4) return updated lead.

userStories.title: Opportunities CRUD + stage history API with tests
userStories.description: Create API routes for Opportunities with automatic stage history recording. When opportunity stage changes, insert a record into opportunity_stage_history. Pipeline view endpoint groups opportunities by stage with totals. Write tests FIRST.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/crm/opportunities.test.ts exists with at least 14 tests

Tests cover: GET list returns opportunities, GET list with division_id filter, GET list with stage filter, GET list with owner_user_id filter, GET list with ?view=pipeline returns grouped data, POST creates opportunity, POST links to lead_id and account_id, POST with invalid data returns 400, GET /[id] returns opportunity with stage_history array, PATCH /[id] updates opportunity, PATCH /[id] changing stage triggers stage_history insert, DELETE /[id] deletes opportunity, pipeline view returns correct totals per stage, PATCH stage from 'intake' to 'closed_lost' records history
app/api/crm/opportunities/route.ts implements GET (with ?view=pipeline option) and POST
app/api/crm/opportunities/[id]/route.ts implements GET (joins stage_history), PATCH (inserts stage_history on stage change), DELETE
Stage history logic: when PATCH changes stage field, insert into opportunity_stage_history(opportunity_id, from_stage, to_stage, changed_by)
Pipeline endpoint returns: { stages: { intake: { opportunities: [...], total_value: number, count: number }, site_visit: {...}, ... } }
All new tests pass
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Pipeline view: group by stage, sum estimated_revenue per stage. Return as object keyed by stage name. Stage history insert should happen in the same PATCH handler — detect if 'stage' field changed by comparing before/after.

Tests cover: GET list returns activities, GET list filters by entity (opportunity_id, lead_id, account_id), GET list filters by activity_type, POST creates activity linked to opportunity, POST creates activity linked to lead, POST with no entity ID fails validation, POST with invalid activity_type fails, GET /[id] returns activity, PATCH /[id] updates activity, DELETE /[id] deletes activity
app/api/crm/activities/route.ts implements GET and POST
app/api/crm/activities/[id]/route.ts implements GET, PATCH, DELETE
GET supports query params: opportunity_id, lead_id, account_id, contact_id, activity_type, limit, offset
All new tests pass
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: E2E CHECKPOINT: CRM API full integration test flow
userStories.description: Write a comprehensive integration test that exercises the ENTIRE CRM flow end-to-end: create account → add contacts → create lead → transition through stages → create opportunity from lead → log activities → verify pipeline view. Also test division isolation. This MUST all pass before moving to estimating.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/integration/crm-flow.test.ts exists with at least 12 tests

Test: Full happy path — create account, add primary contact, create lead with division, transition lead new→qualified→estimating, create opportunity linked to lead+account, log call activity on opportunity, verify pipeline shows opportunity in estimating stage
Test: Division isolation — configure mock JWT with division A, create lead in division A (succeeds), attempt to create lead in division B (fails or returns filtered empty)
Test: Validation chain — POST account with empty name (400), POST contact with invalid email (400), POST lead transition new→won (400), POST activity with no entity (400)
Test: Pipeline aggregation — create 3 opportunities in different stages, GET ?view=pipeline returns correct counts and totals per stage
Test: Stage history — transition opportunity through 3 stages, GET includes full history array in chronological order
Test: Search — create 3 accounts with different names, GET ?search=partial returns only matching accounts
Test: Cascading — delete account, verify contacts for that account are also deleted
All CRM API tests pass (total should be 60+)
All pre-existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds (npm run build)
userStories.notes: CHECKPOINT — Do not proceed to US-011 until ALL tests pass and build succeeds. Run: npm run test && npm run typecheck && npm run build. This is the quality gate for the CRM module.

userStories.title: Estimating Zod validators with calculation tests
userStories.description: Create Zod schemas for Estimates and Estimate Lines. Write thorough tests for validation AND calculation logic. The calculation logic (line total, subtotal, tax, total) should be a pure function tested in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/validators/estimating.ts exports: estimateCreateSchema, estimateUpdateSchema, estimateLineCreateSchema, estimateLineUpdateSchema, estimateLineBatchUpdateSchema
lib/estimating/calculations.ts exports: calculateLineTotal(qty, unitCost, markupPct), calculateEstimateTotals(lines[]), generateEstimateNumber(existingCount) — pure functions
estimateCreateSchema requires: division_id (uuid). Optional: opportunity_id, account_id, contact_id (uuid), currency_code (default 'CAD')

estimateLineCreateSchema requires: description (1-500), quantity (> 0), unit_cost (>= 0). Optional: parent_line_id (uuid), line_type (default 'item'), unit, markup_pct (0-100, default 0), is_optional (boolean), sort_order (integer)
estimateLineBatchUpdateSchema: array of line objects with id + updatable fields
__tests__/validators/estimating.test.ts with at least 15 tests — valid data passes, missing required fails, negative quantity fails, markup > 100 fails, etc.
__tests__/lib/estimating/calculations.test.ts with at least 12 tests:
- calculateLineTotal(10, 50, 0) = 500
- calculateLineTotal(10, 50, 15) = 575 (10*50*1.15)
- calculateLineTotal(0, 50, 10) = 0
- calculateLineTotal(1, 0, 10) = 0
- calculateLineTotal(2.5, 33.33, 10) = correctly rounded to 2 decimal places
- calculateEstimateTotals with 3 lines sums correctly
- calculateEstimateTotals excludes is_optional lines from subtotal
- calculateEstimateTotals applies 13% HST for tax_amount
- calculateEstimateTotals with empty lines returns zeros
- generateEstimateNumber(0) = 'EST-2026-001'
- generateEstimateNumber(42) = 'EST-2026-043'
- generateEstimateNumber with different year
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: calculateLineTotal formula: round(quantity * unitCost * (1 + markupPct/100), 2). calculateEstimateTotals: subtotal = sum of non-optional line totals, tax_amount = round(subtotal * 0.13, 2), total_amount = subtotal + tax_amount. All monetary values rounded to 2 decimal places.

userStories.title: Estimates CRUD API with auto-calculation and tests
userStories.description: Create CRUD API for Estimates. Auto-generates estimate_number on create. GET by ID includes all lines. Supports status transitions (draft → review → sent → approved/rejected). Write failing tests FIRST.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/estimates/estimates.test.ts exists with at least 14 tests

Tests cover: GET list returns estimates, GET list filters by division_id, GET list filters by status, GET list filters by opportunity_id, POST creates estimate with auto-generated estimate_number, POST sets default status='draft', POST with invalid division_id returns 400, GET /[id] returns estimate WITH nested lines array, PATCH /[id] updates estimate, PATCH /[id] status transition draft→review works, PATCH /[id] status transition review→sent works, PATCH /[id] rejects invalid transition (draft→approved), DELETE /[id] deletes estimate, DELETE /[id] cascades to lines
app/api/estimates/route.ts implements GET (list) and POST (create with auto-number)
app/api/estimates/[id]/route.ts implements GET (with lines join), PATCH, DELETE
Estimate number auto-generated using generateEstimateNumber() from lib/estimating/calculations.ts
lib/estimating/estimate-status.ts exports ALLOWED_STATUS_TRANSITIONS and validateStatusTransition() — tested in __tests__/lib/estimating/estimate-status.test.ts (at least 6 tests)
All new tests pass
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Status transitions: draft→review, review→sent, review→draft (return to draft), sent→approved, sent→rejected, any→superseded. Reject all other transitions. GET /[id] should join estimate_lines ordered by sort_order and return them nested in the response: { ...estimate, lines: [...] }.

userStories.title: Estimate lines API with batch operations and recalculation
userStories.description: Create API for estimate line items. Support individual CRUD and batch update (PUT replaces all lines). After any line change, recalculate estimate totals (subtotal, tax_amount, total_amount) and update the parent estimate.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/estimates/lines.test.ts exists with at least 14 tests

Tests cover: GET /[id]/lines returns all lines for estimate sorted by sort_order, POST /[id]/lines adds a line and recalculates estimate totals, POST calculates line_total correctly, PUT /[id]/lines batch replaces all lines and recalculates, PATCH /[id]/lines/[lineId] updates a line and recalculates, DELETE /[id]/lines/[lineId] removes line and recalculates, POST with zero quantity (line_total = 0, still valid), POST with markup_pct (correctly applied), batch update with reordered sort_order, recalculation excludes optional lines from subtotal, recalculation applies 13% HST, adding second line updates totals correctly, deleting last line zeroes totals, line with parent_line_id (hierarchical)
app/api/estimates/[id]/lines/route.ts implements GET, POST (single), PUT (batch)
app/api/estimates/[id]/lines/[lineId]/route.ts implements PATCH, DELETE
After every line mutation, lib/estimating/calculations.ts recalculates and updates parent estimate's subtotal_amount, tax_amount, total_amount
All new tests pass
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Recalculation flow: after insert/update/delete line → fetch all lines for estimate → calculateEstimateTotals(lines) → update estimate record with new totals. PUT batch: delete all existing lines for estimate, insert new lines, recalculate. This should be wrapped in a transaction or use upsert pattern.

userStories.title: Estimate versioning API with snapshot tests
userStories.description: Create API for estimate versioning. Creating a version snapshots the full estimate + all lines as JSON, increments revision_no. Write tests that verify snapshot completeness and revision tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/api/estimates/versions.test.ts exists with at least 8 tests

Tests cover: GET /[id]/versions returns empty array for new estimate, POST /[id]/versions creates snapshot (returns version object), POST snapshot contains full estimate data, POST snapshot contains all line items, POST increments estimate.revision_no from 1 to 2, second POST increments to 3, POST with reason field stores reason, GET /[id]/versions returns versions sorted by revision_no desc, version snapshot is immutable (frozen point-in-time)
app/api/estimates/[id]/versions/route.ts implements GET (list) and POST (create snapshot)
Snapshot JSONB structure: { estimate: {...}, lines: [...], created_at: ISO string }
Creating version updates estimate.revision_no = current + 1
All new tests pass
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Version creation flow: 1) Fetch estimate + lines, 2) Build snapshot JSON, 3) Insert into estimate_versions(estimate_id, revision_no, snapshot, reason, created_by), 4) Update estimate SET revision_no = revision_no + 1.

userStories.title: E2E CHECKPOINT: Estimating full integration test flow
userStories.description: Write comprehensive integration tests for the full estimating workflow. This is the quality gate before moving to UI work.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/integration/estimating-flow.test.ts exists with at least 10 tests

Test: Full happy path — create estimate, add 3 lines (labor, material, equipment), verify totals calculated, create version, update line quantities, verify totals recalculated, create second version, list versions (2 returned)
Test: Estimate number uniqueness — create 2 estimates, verify different numbers (EST-2026-001, EST-2026-002)
Test: Optional lines excluded from totals — add optional line, verify subtotal doesn't include it
Test: Status transitions — draft→review→sent→approved path works, draft→approved rejected
Test: Zero quantity line — valid, line_total = 0, doesn't break totals
Test: Markup calculation — 10 qty * $100 unit cost * 15% markup = $1,150 line total
Test: HST calculation — $10,000 subtotal → $1,300 tax → $11,300 total
Test: Delete estimate cascades to lines and versions
Test: Batch line update replaces all lines correctly
Test: Rounding — verify no floating point errors (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 ≠ 0.30000000000000004)
All estimating tests pass (total should be 45+)
All CRM tests still pass
All pre-existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds (npm run build)
userStories.notes: CHECKPOINT — Do not proceed to US-016 until ALL tests pass and build succeeds. Total test count at this point should be 100+.

userStories.title: CRM data hooks and navigation layout
userStories.description: Create React Query hooks for all CRM data fetching and add CRM section to sidebar navigation. Create CRM layout with sub-navigation tabs. No page content yet — just the hooks and layout shell.
acceptanceCriteria: hooks/useCRM.ts exports: useAccounts(filters?), useAccount(id), useCreateAccount(), useUpdateAccount(), useDeleteAccount(), useContacts(filters?), useContact(id), useCreateContact(), useUpdateContact(), useDeleteContact(), useLeads(filters?), useLead(id), useCreateLead(), useUpdateLead(), useDeleteLead(), useLeadStageTransition(), useOpportunities(filters?), useOpportunity(id), useCreateOpportunity(), useUpdateOpportunity(), usePipeline(), useActivities(filters?), useCreateActivity()

hooks/useEstimates.ts exports: useEstimates(filters?), useEstimate(id), useCreateEstimate(), useUpdateEstimate(), useDeleteEstimate(), useEstimateLines(estimateId), useAddEstimateLine(), useBatchUpdateLines(), useDeleteEstimateLine(), useEstimateVersions(estimateId), useCreateEstimateVersion()
CRM added to navigation items in sidebar/header components — icon: Users or Building2, label: 'CRM', href: '/crm'
Estimates added to navigation — icon: Calculator, label: 'Estimates', href: '/estimates'
app/(dashboard)/crm/layout.tsx exists — sub-navigation tabs: Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities
__tests__/hooks/useCRM.test.ts exists with at least 6 tests (verify hooks call correct API endpoints)
__tests__/hooks/useEstimates.test.ts exists with at least 4 tests
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Use the existing apiFetch from lib/api-client.ts for data fetching in hooks. If React Query is not installed, use SWR or the existing fetching pattern from hooks/useDashboard.ts. Check what data fetching library is already in use. CRM sub-nav should be tabs at the top of the CRM section, not a nested sidebar.

userStories.title: Leads list + detail pages with component tests
userStories.description: Build the Leads list page (table with search, filters) and Lead detail page (info card, stage progression UI, activity timeline, convert-to-opportunity action). Write component tests for key interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(dashboard)/crm/leads/page.tsx — Leads list using ResponsiveTable with columns: lead_name, company_name, stage (badge), estimated_value, assigned_to, created_at
Leads list supports: search input (filters by lead_name), stage dropdown filter, division dropdown filter, 'New Lead' button

app/(dashboard)/crm/leads/[id]/page.tsx — Lead detail with: info card (all fields), stage progression bar showing current stage, stage transition buttons (Next Stage, Mark Lost), activity timeline, 'Convert to Opportunity' action button
components/CRM/StageProgressBar.tsx — reusable stage visualization component
components/CRM/ActivityTimeline.tsx — reusable timeline showing activities chronologically
__tests__/components/CRM/StageProgressBar.test.tsx with at least 4 tests (renders stages, highlights current, handles all stages)
__tests__/components/CRM/ActivityTimeline.test.tsx with at least 3 tests (renders activities, empty state, formats dates)
__tests__/pages/crm/leads.test.tsx with at least 4 tests (renders list, shows empty state, filter controls present, new lead button present)
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use existing ResponsiveTable (components/ui/responsive-table.tsx) for list view. Stage badges: new=gray, qualified=blue, estimating=yellow, proposal_sent=purple, won=green, lost=red. Use shadcn/ui Badge with variant colors.

userStories.title: Accounts + Contacts + Opportunities pages with component tests
userStories.description: Build remaining CRM pages: Accounts list + detail (with contacts tab), Contacts list, Opportunities pipeline view. Write component tests for the pipeline view.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(dashboard)/crm/accounts/page.tsx — Accounts list with search, division filter, 'New Account' button

app/(dashboard)/crm/accounts/[id]/page.tsx — Account detail with tabs: Overview (account info), Contacts (list of contacts for this account with inline add), Activities, Opportunities (linked opportunities)
app/(dashboard)/crm/contacts/page.tsx — Contacts list with search, account filter
app/(dashboard)/crm/opportunities/page.tsx — Pipeline view: columns per stage, opportunity cards showing name/value/probability/close date, stage column headers with count and total value
components/CRM/PipelineView.tsx — reusable pipeline board component
components/CRM/OpportunityCard.tsx — card component for pipeline view

__tests__/components/CRM/PipelineView.test.tsx with at least 5 tests (renders stages as columns, places opportunities in correct columns, shows column totals, handles empty stages, handles empty pipeline)
__tests__/components/CRM/OpportunityCard.test.tsx with at least 3 tests (renders name/value, formats currency, shows probability)
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Pipeline view: simple CSS grid/flex columns, one per stage. No drag-and-drop (keep it simple). Click opportunity card to navigate to detail page. Opportunity card formats currency as CAD. Account detail tabs use shadcn/ui Tabs component.

userStories.title: Estimate builder + line editor UI with component tests
userStories.description: Build the estimate list page and full estimate builder with inline line editing, auto-calculating totals, status transitions, and version history panel. Write component tests for the line editor and totals display.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(dashboard)/estimates/page.tsx — Estimates list with search, status filter, division filter, 'New Estimate' button

app/(dashboard)/estimates/[id]/page.tsx — Full estimate builder page with: header (estimate number, status badge, status transition buttons), client info (account/contact), line items table (inline editable), totals panel (subtotal, HST 13%, total), version history sidebar, 'Save Version' button with reason dialog

components/Estimates/LineItemEditor.tsx — table with editable rows: description, quantity, unit_cost, markup_pct, line_total (auto-calc, read-only), sort handle, delete button, 'Add Line' row at bottom
components/Estimates/TotalsPanel.tsx — displays subtotal, tax (labeled 'HST 13%'), total with CAD currency formatting
components/Estimates/VersionHistory.tsx — list of versions with revision_no, reason, created_at, expandable to show snapshot
__tests__/components/Estimates/LineItemEditor.test.tsx with at least 6 tests (renders lines, add line, edit quantity recalculates, delete line, empty state, sorts by sort_order)
__tests__/components/Estimates/TotalsPanel.test.tsx with at least 4 tests (formats currency, shows correct HST, handles zero, large numbers)
__tests__/components/Estimates/VersionHistory.test.tsx with at least 3 tests (renders versions, empty state, expandable snapshot)
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Line item editing: use controlled inputs. On blur or Enter, save changes via API. Calculate line_total client-side for immediate feedback: qty * unit_cost * (1 + markup/100). TotalsPanel recomputes from all lines in state. Format: new Intl.NumberFormat('en-CA', { style: 'currency', currency: 'CAD' }).

userStories.title: E2E CHECKPOINT: Full UI build + all component tests pass
userStories.description: Final quality gate for UI. Verify ALL component tests pass, full build succeeds, typecheck clean. No broken imports, no dead code.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test passes — ALL tests (should be 150+ at this point)
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run lint passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds with zero errors
No unused imports or dead code in new CRM/Estimates files
All CRM pages accessible via navigation (manually verify routes exist)
All Estimate pages accessible via navigation
StageProgressBar, ActivityTimeline, PipelineView, OpportunityCard, LineItemEditor, TotalsPanel, VersionHistory all render without errors
Total test count documented in progress.txt
userStories.notes: CHECKPOINT — Do not proceed to US-022 until ALL checks pass. This is the final UI quality gate. Run all 4 commands sequentially and verify each passes.

userStories.title: ERP sync mock infrastructure with comprehensive tests
userStories.description: Build ERPNext sync infrastructure with mock mode. Sync service, mock response generator, sync API endpoint. Account→Customer and Estimate→Quotation mapping. Thorough tests for sync flow.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/erp/sync-service.ts exports: SyncService class with syncAccount(accountId), syncEstimate(estimateId), getSyncStatus(entityType, entityId) methods
lib/erp/mock-responses.ts exports: mockCustomerResponse(account), mockQuotationResponse(estimate, lines) — generate realistic ERPNext API response shapes
lib/erp/client.ts updated: isMockMode() check (true when ERPNEXT_BASE_URL empty or equals 'mock'), createCustomer(data) and createQuotation(data) methods that delegate to mock or real
app/api/erp/sync/route.ts implements POST { entity_type, entity_id } — triggers sync, returns sync job status
app/api/erp/sync/[jobId]/route.ts implements GET — returns sync job status

Sync flow: 1) Create erp_sync_jobs record (status=queued), 2) Call ERPNext API (or mock), 3) Update job status, 4) Create erp_sync_map entry linking local ID to ERPNext docname, 5) Log erp_sync_events
Account→Customer mapping: account_name→customer_name, account_type→customer_type, billing_address→primary_address
Estimate→Quotation mapping: estimate lines→quotation items, totals→quotation totals, contact→party

__tests__/lib/erp/sync-service.test.ts with at least 10 tests: syncAccount creates job + mock customer + sync map entry, syncEstimate creates mock quotation, getSyncStatus returns correct status, mock mode doesn't call real fetch, sync handles error gracefully (status=failed), sync records events, sync map links correct IDs, retry logic (attempt_count increments), concurrent syncs don't conflict, sync for already-synced entity updates existing map
__tests__/api/erp/sync.test.ts with at least 4 tests: POST triggers sync, GET returns status, POST with invalid entity returns 400, POST requires auth
All new tests pass
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Mock mode detection: const isMock = !process.env.ERPNEXT_BASE_URL || process.env.ERPNEXT_BASE_URL === 'mock'. In mock mode, generate deterministic fake ERPNext docnames like 'CUST-MOCK-001'. Real mode will use the existing ErpClient.create() method.

userStories.title: Division isolation + edge case integration tests
userStories.description: Final edge case test suite. Tests that validate security boundaries (division isolation via RLS), calculation edge cases, and data integrity. These catch bugs that individual unit tests miss.
acceptanceCriteria: __tests__/integration/division-isolation.test.ts with at least 6 tests:
- User with division 'contracting' can list leads in 'contracting' division
- User with division 'contracting' gets empty result for 'homes' division leads
- User with multiple divisions sees data from all their divisions
- Platform admin sees all divisions regardless of JWT claims
- Creating account in a division user doesn't belong to fails
- Estimate in division A not visible to user in division B
__tests__/integration/calculation-edge-cases.test.ts with at least 6 tests:
- Estimate with 100 lines calculates correctly (stress test)
- Line with quantity=0.0001 and unit_cost=99999.99 doesn't overflow
- Batch update of 50 lines recalculates totals correctly
- Deleting all lines sets estimate totals to 0
- Markup of exactly 100% doubles the line total
- Currency formatting handles large numbers ($1,234,567.89)
__tests__/integration/data-integrity.test.ts with at least 4 tests:
- Deleting an account cascades to its contacts
- Deleting an estimate cascades to lines and versions
- Opportunity stage history is immutable (no update/delete endpoints)
- Estimate version snapshot is immutable (no update/delete endpoints)
All tests pass (total should be 170+)
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Division isolation tests use different mock JWT claims to simulate different users. Platform admin test uses krewpact_roles=['platform_admin']. These tests validate the RLS policy design from US-003.
